[0mpurpose is demonstrating the least-trusted [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordain]8;;\[0m[0m mode. You[0m
[0mbuild a trivial image locally, graft it, and read the [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch]8;;\[0m[0m[0m
[0mverdict that honestly reports GRAFTED — provenance stops at the[0m
[0mlocal machine.[0m

[0mPrerequisites:[0m

[0m[32m [*] [0m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Director\Director]8;;\[0m[0m credential installed[0m
[0m[32m [*] [0m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot]8;;\[0m[0m configured (RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID populated)[0m
[0m[32m [*] [0m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\rbev-graft-demo]8;;\[0m[0m [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Regime\Regime]8;;\[0m[0m present (RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=graft)[0m

[1;37mWhy Graft exists[0m

[0m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Graft\Graft]8;;\[0m[0m is the development and prototyping path — you have an[0m
[0mimage on your workstation, you want it in the [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot]8;;\[0m[0m, and[0m
[0mforcing a Cloud Build roundtrip would slow you down. The trade[0m
[0mis honest: the resulting [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark]8;;\[0m[0m carries no SLSA chain[0m
[0mbecause Cloud Build never ran.[0m

[0mThree [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordain]8;;\[0m[0m modes, three trust contracts:[0m

[0m  [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure]8;;\[0m[0m  Cloud Build constructs the image from the project's[0m
[0m            Dockerfile. Trust the build chain (SLSA [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Provenance\Provenance]8;;\[0m[0m).[0m
[0m  [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bind\Bind]8;;\[0m[0m     Mirror an upstream image by digest. Trust the[0m
[0m            upstream publisher; the digest pins what you got.[0m
[0m  [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Graft\Graft]8;;\[0m[0m    Push a locally-built image. Trust the local machine[0m
[0m            and the [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Director\Director]8;;\[0m[0m who operated it.[0m

[0m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Graft\Graft]8;;\[0m[0m is not a failure mode — it is the mode you pick when[0m
[0ma locally-built image is the right answer. [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch]8;;\[0m[0m will report[0m
[0mGRAFTED so anyone reading the [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot]8;;\[0m[0m downstream can see the[0m
[0mtrust level at a glance.[0m

[1;37mStep 1 — [1;37mBuild a local image[0m

   [0m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\rbev-graft-demo]8;;\[0m[0m ships with no Dockerfile. The [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel]8;;\[0m[0m [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Regime\Regime]8;;\[0m[0m[0m
   [0mdeclares RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=graft and names the local image tag the[0m
   [0m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Graft\Graft]8;;\[0m[0m operation will push — everything else about the image[0m
   [0mis your concern. Any local Docker image works; we use busybox as[0m
   [0ma deliberately trivial target so the focus stays on the trust[0m
   [0mmodel, not the image contents.[0m

   [0mPull and tag busybox under a teaching identifier:[0m

   [36m   docker pull busybox:latest[0m
   [36m   docker tag busybox:latest graft-busybox:local[0m

   [0mThen open [36mrbev-vessels/rbev-graft-demo/rbrv.env[0m and set [36mRBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE[0m to the tag you chose:[0m

   [36m   RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE=graft-busybox:local[0m

   [0m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Graft\Graft]8;;\[0m[0m reads this value and looks up that tag in your local[0m
   [0mDocker image cache at [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordain]8;;\[0m[0m time. The tag is the handoff[0m
   [0mbetween what you built and what the [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot]8;;\[0m[0m receives.[0m

[1;37mStep 2 — [1;37m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Graft\Graft]8;;\[0m[1;37m — push the local image[0m

   [0m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordain]8;;\[0m[0m reads RBRV_VESSEL_MODE from the [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#RBRV\RBRV]8;;\[0m[0m and routes[0m
   [0maccordingly. For [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Graft\Graft]8;;\[0m[0m, that means: look up RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE[0m
   [0min your local Docker image cache, tag it for the [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot]8;;\[0m[0m's[0m
   [0mGAR repository, and push. No Dockerfile is read. No [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Pouch\Pouch]8;;\[0m[0m[0m
   [0mis assembled. No Cloud Build is triggered.[0m

   [0mThe [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark]8;;\[0m[0m format encodes two timestamps: the image's own[0m
   [0mcreation time (from [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot]8;;\[0m[0m layer metadata) and the push time.[0m
   [0mBoth come from the host — there is no server-side notary.[0m

   [0m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordain]8;;\[0m[0m:[0m

   [0m   [36mtt/rbw-fO.DirectorOrdainsHallmark.sh rbev-graft-demo[0m[0m

   [0mWall-clock: seconds. The only network traffic is the push itself;[0m
   [0mthe image is already on your machine.[0m

[1;37mStep 3 — [1;37mInspect the [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch]8;;\[0m[1;37m verdict[0m

   [0m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch]8;;\[0m[0m is mode-aware. For [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjured]8;;\[0m[0m [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmarks]8;;\[0m[0m it verifies[0m
   [0mthe SLSA [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Provenance\Provenance]8;;\[0m[0m chain. For [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bind\Bind]8;;\[0m[0m it attests the[0m
   [0mdigest-pin relationship to the upstream image. For [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Graft\Graft]8;;\[0m[0m[0m
   [0mthere is nothing to verify — no build record, no upstream digest,[0m
   [0mjust an image the [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Director\Director]8;;\[0m[0m asserted is theirs. The verdict[0m
   [0mrecords that honestly as GRAFTED.[0m

   [0m[97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch]8;;\[0m[0m the [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmarks]8;;\[0m[0m:[0m

   [0m   [36mtt/rbw-fV.DirectorVouchesHallmarks.sh[0m[0m

   [0mThen [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tally\Tally]8;;\[0m[0m to read each [97;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark]8;;\[0m[0m's health:[0m

params.intent: Land the graft-mode onboarding handbook track: new rbho_director_graft() in rbhodg_director_graft.sh implements the 3-step teaching arc (build a local busybox image and edit RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE, graft via ordain to push the local image to the Depot, inspect the GRAFTED Vouch verdict). Teaching target rbev-graft-demo (landed ₢A6AAH) carries RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=graft and no project Dockerfile — the track frames graft as the least-trusted ordain mode where SLSA cannot cover the image because Cloud Build never ran, and the Vouch verdict reads GRAFTED as an honest signal that provenance stops at the local machine. Four filesystem probes (director credential, depot configured, rbev-graft-demo vessel regime present with mode=graft, grafted hallmark in local docker cache via g-prefix tag match) render inline as the learner progresses. Dedicated 'Why Graft exists' section between Prerequisites and Step 1 positions graft as the development/prototyping path (local image, you want it in the Depot, Cloud Build roundtrip would slow you down) and presents the three-mode trust hierarchy side by side: Conjure (Cloud Build, SLSA), Bind (upstream digest pin), Graft (local machine, GRAFTED). Closing 'What you learned' section returns to the trust hierarchy as a Vouch-verdict-readable summary and calls out explicitly that rbev-graft-demo has no Nameplate and never runs in a Crucible — it is pure teaching contrast, and production graft usage follows the same pattern (RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=graft, RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE at a local tag, ordain, vouch records GRAFTED). Extensive RBYC_* linked-term usage throughout: ORDAIN/CONJURE/BIND/GRAFT/VOUCH/TALLY operations, VESSEL/HALLMARK/HALLMARKS/DEPOT/POUCH/NAMEPLATE/CRUCIBLE/SENTRY artifacts, DIRECTOR/PAYOR roles, RBRV/REGIME regime references, PROBE_YES/NO, SBOM/PROVENANCE/CONJURED. Thin dispatcher tt/rbw-Odg.OnboardingDirectorGraft.sh uses the nolog launcher (byte-identical to the Oda/Odb dispatchers — 159 bytes). Zipper enrollment in rbz_zipper.sh adds RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_GRAFT (colophon rbw-Odg, empty channel) between RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_BIND and RBZ_ONBOARD_PAYOR_HB. CLI furnish in rbho0_cli.sh sources the new helper after rbhodb. Start-here menu entry in rbho0_start_here.sh rewrites the prior stub Graft entry into a fully-wired entry parallel to Bind: refines the brief to name Director as the build owner and frame Vouch verdict GRAFTED inline, three-bullet Steps list mirroring the teaching arc (Build a local image / Graft — push to Depot / Inspect Vouch verdict), Requires line names Director credentials + provisioned Depot + rbev-graft-demo vessel present as the concrete prerequisites (matching the in-handbook probe set), and ends with buh_tt linking RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_GRAFT so learners can launch the handbook directly from the menu. Docket staleness noted and handled: docket called for sequence in rbho_onboarding.sh but decomposition (₢A6AAh) split that file into Tools/rbk/rbh0/ one-per-file — followed the current rbhod{f,a,b}_ pattern so the new file is rbhodg_director_graft.sh. Generated tabtarget context regenerated via MarshalGenerate shows rbw-Odg as 'Graft Cloud Build — push locally-built image, inspect GRAFTED Vouch verdict' in the Onboarding group between Oda and Odf. Dry-renders of both tt/rbw-Odg.OnboardingDirectorGraft.sh and tt/rbw-o.ONBOARDING.sh confirm the teaching prose, three-mode trust-contract table, and menu entry land cleanly; all RBYC_* linked terms resolve against current repo state; the three prereq probes fire correctly (director credential, depot, vessel regime all PASS on this station) and the grafted-hallmark probe correctly renders PROBE_NO (no graft has yet been pushed to this station's Depot).
